overview.tagline: Bergen County's largest township — where the Ramapo Mountains meet suburban convenience
overview.description: Mahwah is the largest municipality in Bergen County by area, occupying the northwest corner where New Jersey meets New York State. The township is defined by its dramatic natural landscape, with the Ramapo Mountains and Ramapo River providing stunning scenery and abundant outdoor recreation. Home to Ramapo College of New Jersey and the former Ford Motor Company headquarters, Mahwah offers a unique blend of mountain living, suburban neighborhoods, and corporate campuses, all connected by excellent highway access via Route 17 and Interstate 287.
highlights: Bergen County's largest township with dramatic Ramapo Mountain scenery
Home to Ramapo College of New Jersey
Campgaw Mountain Reservation for skiing, hiking, and camping
Excellent highway access via Route 17, I-287, and I-87 (NY Thruway)
Mix of mountain, suburban, and corporate campus environments

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Mahwah's large geography creates significant variation in home values from mountain properties to suburban neighborhoods.
realEstate.recentTrends: Mahwah's housing market is driven by demand for its natural setting, strong schools, and highway access. The township offers everything from modest ranches to luxury mountain estates. Proximity to corporate offices and Ramapo College also fuels demand. New luxury developments have added inventory in recent years.
neighborhoodNotes: Cragmere Park — Established neighborhood near the center of town with well-maintained homes
Ramapo Valley — Properties near the Ramapo River with mountain views
Fardale section — Family-friendly neighborhood near Fardale Elementary School
Mountain area — Larger lots and wooded properties in the Ramapo Mountains
Route 17 corridor — Mix of commercial and residential near shopping

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided by a private hauler contracted by the Township of Mahwah.
details: Pickup occurs once or twice per week depending on zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule.
details: Commingled recyclables collected on scheduled days
Paper and cardboard collected separately
Recycling drop-off available at the township recycling center
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer service is managed by the Mahwah Township Department of Public Works.
details: Water supplied from local wells and the Ramapo Valley watershed
Sewer service maintained by the Township DPW
Quarterly billing for water and sewer service
Some mountain properties on private well and septic systems
Water quality reports published annually

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Environmental permits may be required for properties in sensitive areas
Septic system permits for properties not on municipal sewer
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Township manages municipal parking lots and commuter parking near transit areas.
details: Commuter parking available at the Mahwah train station
Municipal lots available near the township center
Parking at Campgaw Mountain and hiking trailheads
Overnight parking restrictions on certain township roads
Snow emergency parking bans enforced

additionalServices.title: Mahwah Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library with extensive programs and resources.
details: Located at 100 Ridge Road
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Large meeting rooms available for community use
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Extensive recreational programs taking advantage of Mahwah's natural resources.
details: Youth sports leagues, hiking programs, and summer camps
Campgaw Mountain skiing and snowboarding programs
Adult fitness, tennis, and outdoor programs
Senior citizen programs and activities
Winter Lodge and Ramapo Valley recreation areas

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Mahwah enforces noise regulations to protect the township's residential and natural character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment and leaf blowers restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk and driveway snow removal required within 24 hours
Properties must be kept free of debris
Abandoned vehicles must be removed
Wooded properties must maintain fire safety clearances

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Mahwah requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws, with special attention to wildlife encounters.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed on all public property and trails
Pet waste must be picked up immediately, including on trails
Bear-proof garbage containers recommended in mountain areas
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated throughout the township including trailhead and commuter areas.
keyRules: Commuter parking at the train station requires a valid permit
Overnight parking restricted on many township roads
Trailhead and park parking subject to posted hours
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles restricted on residential streets

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with township codes.
keyRules: Rental properties should be registered with the Township
Properties must meet housing and fire safety codes
Landlords responsible for maintaining rental units
Inspections may be conducted at change of tenancy
Short-term rentals subject to local regulations

other.title: Environmental Protection
other.summary: Mahwah has strong environmental regulations to protect its mountain and watershed areas.
keyRules: Development restricted in environmentally sensitive areas
Tree removal permits required for significant trees
Steep slope ordinance limits construction on hillsides
Watershed protection regulations apply near the Ramapo River
Wildlife management guidelines in mountain areas

other.title: Bear Awareness
other.summary: Mahwah's mountain location means black bear encounters are possible, and residents must take precautions.
keyRules: Garbage must be stored in bear-resistant containers in mountain areas
Bird feeders should be removed during bear season (spring through fall)
Pet food should not be left outdoors
Report bear sightings to the NJ Division of Fish & Wildlife
other.source: Township of Mahwah / NJ Division of Fish & Wildlife

publicSchools.name: Mahwah High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with strong academics, outdoor education programs, and competitive athletics

publicSchools.name: Ramapo Ridge Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving all township students

publicSchools.name: Betsy Ross Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the Cragmere Park section

publicSchools.name: Commodore Perry Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the central part of the township

publicSchools.name: Fardale Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the Fardale section near Route 202

publicSchools.name: Joyce Kilmer Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the eastern portion of the township

privateSchools.name: Immaculate Heart of Mary School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving the Mahwah community

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — in town

whyMoveHere: Stunning Ramapo Mountain scenery with hiking, skiing, and outdoor recreation
Bergen County's largest township with spacious lots and varied terrain
Home to Ramapo College of New Jersey
Excellent highway access via Route 17, I-287, and I-87
Strong public school district with good extracurricular programs
Campgaw Mountain Reservation for year-round outdoor activities

commuteToNYC.duration: 55-75 minutes to Hoboken by train, then PATH to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.notes: The Mahwah train station provides service on the NJ Transit Main Line to Hoboken Terminal, where riders can connect to Manhattan via the PATH train or NY Waterway ferry. Bus service along Route 17 is also available. Many commuters also drive to Suffern for additional Metro-North options or to Route 17 park-and-ride lots.

costOfLiving.summary: Mahwah offers a wide range of housing options from moderate to luxury, with costs varying significantly between suburban neighborhoods and mountain properties.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 30% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Beautiful mountain scenery and abundant outdoor recreation
Spacious properties with many wooded lots
Good schools with strong community support
Excellent highway access for commuters heading to NYC or North Jersey
Diverse housing options from starter homes to luxury estates
cons: Long commute to Manhattan compared to southern Bergen County towns
Bear and deer encounters common in mountain areas
Significant distance from Bergen County's cultural amenities
Mountain properties may have well and septic systems
Winter weather can be more severe in mountain elevations

restaurants.name: River Palm Terrace
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse offering prime steaks, fresh seafood, and an extensive wine list in an elegant setting.

restaurants.name: Mason Jar
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant and bar serving American comfort food, craft beers, and cocktails in a casual, modern atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Il Villagio
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering classic pasta dishes, seafood, and wood-fired pizza in a warm, family-friendly setting.

restaurants.name: Mahwah Bar & Grill
restaurants.description: Local bar and grill offering pub fare, burgers, and a selection of beers in a neighborhood setting.

parks.name: Campgaw Mountain Reservation
parks.description: Bergen County park and ski area featuring downhill skiing and snowboarding in winter, plus hiking trails, camping, and archery year-round. The highest point in Bergen County.

parks.name: Ramapo Valley County Reservation
parks.description: 1,168-acre county park with extensive hiking trails through Ramapo Mountain forests, waterfalls, lake, and scenic overlooks.

parks.name: Darlington County Park
parks.description: Popular Bergen County park with a large swimming lake, picnic areas, sports fields, and a nature trail.

parks.name: Joyce Kilmer County Park
parks.description: County park with playgrounds, athletic fields, and walking paths in a wooded setting.

events.name: Mahwah Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring live music, food vendors, rides, games, and fireworks at Commodore Perry Field.
events.when: September, typically a Saturday in mid-September

events.name: Campgaw Mountain Winter Season
events.description: Downhill skiing and snowboarding at Campgaw Mountain Reservation, with night skiing available.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade & Ceremony
events.description: Annual Memorial Day observance with a parade and ceremony honoring veterans.

events.name: Ramapo College Cultural Events
events.description: Concerts, theater productions, and cultural events open to the public at the Ramapo College campus.

attractions.name: Campgaw Mountain Reservation
attractions.description: Bergen County's premier outdoor recreation destination, offering skiing, snowboarding, hiking, camping, and archery across 1,351 acres of mountain terrain.

attractions.name: Ramapo College of New Jersey
attractions.description: Public liberal arts college with a beautiful 300-acre campus in the Ramapo Mountains. The campus hosts cultural events, art galleries, and nature trails open to the public.

attractions.name: Ramapo Valley County Reservation
attractions.description: Expansive nature reserve with miles of hiking trails, scenic overlooks, Scarlet Oak Pond, and diverse forest habitats.

shoppingAreas: Route 17 North — Major retail corridor with shopping centers
Mahwah Town Center — Shops and services near the municipal center
Ramsey Town Center — Nearby shops and restaurants
Woodbury Common Premium Outlets (NY) — 20 minutes away

government.meetingSchedule: Township Council meets the 2nd and 4th Thursday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Municipal Building, 475 Corporate Drive, Mahwah, NJ 07430

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
NJ Division of Fish & Wildlife (Bear Reports)
